author: zhanmyz
title: Smart CI fails: fastcore 2.0.1 breaks ghapi 1.0.4 - L.starmap AttributeError

failed_jobs.name: Smart_CI
failed_jobs.symptom: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap' when constructing GhApi()

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_ubuntu_24
failed_jobs.symptom: Downstream status check exits 1 due to Smart_CI failure

key_errors:
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ghapi/core.py", line 105, in __init__
    funcs_ = L(funcs).starmap(_GhVerb, client=self, kwargs=kwargs)
             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/fastcore/basics.py", line 578, in __getattr__
    if attr is not None: return getattr(attr,k)
                               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'
root_cause: The smart-ci requirements.txt pins ghapi==1.0.4 but specifies fastcore>=1.5.4 with no upper bound. fastcore 2.0.1 was resolved and installed, but it is incompatible with ghapi 1.0.4: the L class no longer provides a 'starmap' method (returns a plain list) that ghapi 1.0.4 depends on in GhApi.__init__ (core.py line 105). This is the 3rd occurrence today (run 29000379233 at 07:11Z affecting PR #36780, run 29000703414 at 07:26Z affecting PR #36773, and now this run 29001747652 at 07:40Z affecting PR #36504). Fix: pin fastcore to fastcore>=1.5.4,<2.0.0 in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt, or upgrade ghapi to a version that supports fastcore 2.x.
reproduction_steps: pip install ghapi==1.0.4 fastcore==2.0.1; python3 -c "from ghapi.all import GhApi; GhApi()"
recommended_actions: Pin fastcore in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt to fastcore>=1.5.4,<2.0.0 to prevent future breaks from fastcore major version bumps.
Alternatively, upgrade ghapi to a version that supports fastcore 2.x (check ghapi releases for compatibility).
This is a recurring infrastructure failure blocking multiple PRs — escalation/immediate fix is needed.
prevention_strategies: Always specify upper bounds for transitive dependencies in requirements.txt files used by CI infrastructure scripts. Consider using pip-compile or a lockfile. Add a CI sanity-check step that imports the smart-ci module before running it.
historical_context: This is the 3rd occurrence of this failure on 2026-07-09. First occurrence: run 29000379233 (PR #36780, ~07:11Z). Second occurrence: run 29000703414 (PR #36773, Linux Ubuntu 22.04 ARM64 cross-compilation, ~07:26Z). All three runs failed within 30 minutes, indicating the fastcore 2.0.1 release is persistently breaking the ghapi 1.0.4 API for all merge-queue CI workflows.
